mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above its 12-month trend, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This dual signal of persistent monetary tightness predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% AND CPIAUCSL growth rate > 3% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% near restrictive threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between the interaction signal and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% and remains elevated for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 4.2% near trigger threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE > 4.5 episodes are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% annualized, signaling weakening consumer demand that compresses near-term corporate revenue growth and equity valuations. This demand weakness produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth rate falls below 0.15% (annualized < 1.8%)
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following weak RSAFS signals are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC between RSAFS growth and forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expands consistently above its 3-month trend, indicating robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support corporate profit margins and equity valuations. This supply strength produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods of rising INDPRO are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% with t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) both contract simultaneously, indicating synchronized weakness across residential and consumer demand channels. This dual demand shock predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month in same month
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following dual demand shocks are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction signal falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100,000 jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, signaling a transition from labor market tightness to slack. This leading indicator of demand weakness predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 83,000 jobs (100k annualized pace) for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following weak PAYEMS growth are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate declines by more than 50 basis points over a 3-month window while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains above 3%, signaling a shift toward monetary accommodation despite persistent price pressures. This policy pivot reduces discount rates and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines > 0.50pp over 3 months AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% and CPIAUCSL at 319.615
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following this policy pivot signal are negative in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth decelerates to below 1.5% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.4 million units annualized, indicating synchronized weakness in aggregate demand and residential investment. This dual slowdown predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession risk reprices.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 1.5% AND HOUST < 1.4 million units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 23542.349 billion chained 2017 dollars
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession regime
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following this dual slowdown signal are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
